title: Sleepy Lamp
bio: Nora Glowblink is a sleepy lamp who keeps nodding off during midnight doodles. She feels dreamy, collects moth compliments, and flickers little stars when she yawns.

steps.title: Base Shapes
steps.instruction: On the Guides layer, draw a trapezoid-like lampshade using simple slanted sides. Add a thin rectangle stem and a flat oval base.
steps.hint: Start with big simple shapes before adding any glow.

steps.title: Face Placement
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, draw two sleepy curved eyes on the lampshade. Add soft droopy eyebrows and a tiny yawn mouth.
steps.hint: Curved closed eyes instantly make the lamp look tired.

steps.title: Personality Details
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add a pull chain with a tiny star charm, small glow lines, and one little moth-shaped oval nearby.
steps.hint: The moth can be just a small oval body with two teardrop wings.

steps.title: Clean Lineart
steps.instruction: On the Lineart layer, trace the shade, stem, base, sleepy face, pull chain, and moth. Keep the glow lines light and simple.
steps.hint: Clean outlines help the lamp stay readable even with cute details.

steps.title: Color & Details
steps.instruction: On the Color + Details layer, fill the lampshade, stem, and base. Add pale glow around the shade and a soft oval shadow below.
steps.hint: Use a lighter color near the middle of the shade to make Nora feel gently lit.
